bulk.confirm: Queue {n} items? They'll run sequentially — Orchestrator takes one run at a time.
bulk.queue.title: Queue
bulk.queue.clear_confirm: Clear the whole queue? Already-dispatched runs continue to live in the DB — this just removes their cards from this panel.
bulk.queue.retry_force_confirm: Retry failed items with force=1? This bypasses the evolution conflict gate and will apply them even when multiple proposals target the same agent.
bulk.queue.diag.blocked_hint: A live Orchestrator run is holding the project's single slot. Queue will resume after it finishes.
bulk.queue.diag.stopped_hint: Background task isn't running but there are pending items. Click "wake dispatcher".
tile.orchestration.pending_hint: Items queued for sequential Orchestrator dispatch
projects.title: Projects
projects.slug_hint: a-z, 0-9 and hyphen; starts and ends with a letter/digit
projects.legend_save: ✏ Save — press {enter} next to the field (or Enter inside the field).
projects.legend_slug: Slug changes the URL and scheduler jobs, so it requires confirmation.
projects.confirm_delete: Type slug «{slug}» to confirm project deletion:
projects.slug_change_url: URL changes: /p/{orig}/… → /p/{next}/… — old bookmarks will break.
projects.slug_change_note: Per-project settings and sessions remain (bound to id), scheduler jobs will be re-registered.
setup.title: First-Time Setup
setup.found_count: {n} projects found. Enabled ones will be imported.
setup.scan_hint: After clicking, the page reloads and shows the discovered folders. If nothing is found you'll see a red banner with the reason.
settings.title: Global Settings
settings.project_hint: Per-project overrides. Inherit = uses global; Override = explicit value.
review.title: Triage: items needing attention
review.subtitle: Aggregates proposed evolutions, open high-priority tech-debt items, and recent sidecar findings
sidebar.pick_project: Pick a project from the menu above
topics.editor_hint: Editing and generation live on the Kanban page.
topics.weekly_desc: It's a file listing topics agents will study this week.
topics.location: It lives in the project at
topics.current_empty: It's missing right now — so the Topics page is empty and {code} runs agents without topic hints.
topics.bootstrap_desc: I'll create a template. You can then open the file in an editor and add topics per agent (format {code1} → {code2}).
topics.not_found: Weekly checklist not found. Expected paths:
topics.empty_title: Template file created, but topics are still empty
topics.empty_desc: Open it in an editor and add topics in this format:
topics.empty_hint: After saving, reload this page — you'll see your topics.
topics.empty_skipped: Sections {h1} and {h2} are skipped by the parser — they're only for human notes.
topics.db_subtitle: These will be fed into /self-study tonight. Manage them (add manually, delete, generate) on the Kanban page.
topics.empty_v2_desc: No topics yet. Generate them with the "Generate topics" button on Kanban — or add them manually there.
topics.empty_file_side_channel: The template file stays as a side channel for manual notes (it is not injected into /self-study; Kanban does that):
ideas.col.title: title
ideas.empty.title: No markdown files in {dir}
ideas.empty.desc: Product ideas are written by the {code} slash-command — claude scans the repo for UX TODO comments, «coming soon» stubs, gaps and inconsistencies, then drops each idea as a markdown file here. It runs weekly on schedule (if cron is enabled), or you can trigger it right now.
ideas.detail.confirm_orch: Run Orchestrator with this idea as goal?
ideas.detail.confirm_gh: Create GitHub issue from this idea?
ideas.confirm.orchestrate_list: Run Orchestrator with this idea as goal?
ai_usage.title: AI Usage
ai_usage.context_for_global: For context (no model filter, all projects):
cascade_costs.title: Orchestration cost
cascade_costs.subtitle: USD and tokens across all orchestration runs in the project · JOIN orchestrator_runs ⋈ ai_usage_events
cascade_costs.prices: Prices are approximate ($/1M): opus 15/75, sonnet 3/15, haiku 0.8/4
cascade_costs.empty: No orchestration runs in the selected period yet.
cascade_costs.empty_hint: Costs appear automatically after the first Orchestrator run on the {link} page. Each run accumulates usage events (input/output tokens, tools) — this page aggregates them into dollars and tokens. Cost_usd is read from payload_json of orchestrator_events; tokens come via JOIN ai_usage_events on claude session_id.
contracts.confirm_audit: Run Orchestrator: audit contract «{name}» against real code?
contracts.confirm_gh: Create GitHub issue from this contract?
contracts.empty.title: No contract files in {dir}
contracts.empty.desc: Contracts are written by the {code} slash-command — claude walks the code, picks out modules and pages, describes their public API, dependencies and invariants as markdown with frontmatter. The orchestrator also writes them during feature decomposition.
findings.confirm_orch: Run Orchestrator with this finding as goal?
scan.last.label: Last scan
findings.empty.title: No markdown files in {dir}
findings.empty.desc: Findings (a.k.a. tech-debt items) are written by the {code} slash-command — claude scans the repo for TODO/FIXME/legacy/smells and drops each finding as a markdown file here. You can run it right now:
findings.detail.confirm_orch: Run Orchestrator with this finding as goal?
findings.detail.confirm_gh: Create GitHub issue from this finding?
findings.col.title: title
evolutions.confirm_apply: Run Orchestrator: apply this edit to `.claude/agents/{name}.md`?
evolutions.confirm_apply_force: ⚠ CONFLICT: another open evolution proposal targets agent «{name}». Apply anyway? This may overwrite parallel changes.
evolutions.col.name: name
evolutions.col.title: title
evolutions.confirm_gh: Create GitHub issue from this evolution?
evolutions.rubric.title: Rubric stats
evolutions.rubric.subtitle: Verdict distribution across evolution proposals
evolutions.rubric.no_data_hint: No file has a rubric block yet. Older /evolve-agent versions don't emit them — expected. New proposals with frontmatter rubric: will populate this panel.
plans.confirm_gh: Create GitHub issue from this plan?
plans.empty.title: No plan files in {dir}
plans.empty.desc: Plans are written either by the Orchestrator from {link} during feature decomposition, or by the {code} slash-command — it walks recent commits / TODO comments / spec files and surfaces active in-flight plans with a checklist of tasks.
plans.detail.confirm_continue: Run Orchestrator to continue this plan?
plans.detail.confirm_gh: Create GitHub issue from this plan?
kanban.schedule_subtitle: Schedule for the next 5 days — {n} agents per night
kanban.legend.order: Order: longest-untrained first, then by tier
kanban.placeholder.title: Topic title
kanban.placeholder.question: What exactly to study
kanban.col.title: Title
kanban.no_custom_topics: No custom topics. Add one above — it will be mixed into the nightly_learning prompt.
help.what_desc: AI Dreaming Center is a Claude CLI session orchestrator, split per project. Each row in {code} is a separate micode folder with its own agents, settings and nightly learning schedule.
help.agents_desc: In {dir} — definitions are pulled from there. The left sidebar switches between this project's sections; the project select on top — between projects.
help.useful.settings: • Global defaults live at {settings_link} — per-project overrides at {project_link}.
help.useful.sessions: • Claude CLI sessions are launched by {pm}; their stdout/stderr is written to the DB and streamed via {live}.
help.useful.scheduler: • The schedule (nightly learning) is held by {sched} with per-project jobs.
help.useful.reload: • If something looks broken — first check the uvicorn restart: {reload} doesn't reload the i18n cache on the fly (it's read once at I18n start).
help.section.dashboard: weekly metrics + recent sessions + what's currently running
help.section.orchestration: multi-agent runs: decompose a task, launch an orchestrator session
help.section.questions: questions from agents waiting for your answer
help.section.live: live SSE output of all project processes
help.section.rotation: which agent learns on which day
help.section.kanban: for now — form to add topics; on the roadmap — 5-day plan
help.section.wiki: docs/wiki/ overview — domain coverage and modules
help.section.ai_usage: tokens, charts, breakdown by model and main vs subagents
help.section.loops_templates: catalog of .md templates to run loops
help.section.plans: multi-step plans — what the agent has done / is doing
help.section.settings: per-project overrides on top of global settings
loops.empty.title: No loop files in {dir}
loops.empty.desc: Loops are documentation for recurring processes (CI/CD, release, deploy, weekly review, etc.). They're written by agents in orchestration or by the {code} slash-command — which scans {pkg} and records discovered loops as markdown.
loops_tpl.dir_missing: Folder doesn't exist. Create it to drop .md templates inside.
loops_tpl.col.name: name
loops_tpl.empty: No templates yet. Drop a {ext} file in the folder — it will appear here.
loops_tpl.field.name: Name
questions.empty.title: No questions yet.
questions.empty.desc: When an agent in a running session calls {ask} or POSTs {post} via Bash — the question shows up here, the watchdog pauses the idle timer, you reply, the agent reads the answer via {get} and continues.
questions.confirm_dismiss: Close the question without an answer? The agent will get an empty string.
wiki.bootstrap_hint: Run {code} — Claude CLI will walk the repo and put domain pages here.
wiki_health.col.label: label
notes.empty: No notes. They appear after successful self-study sessions (written to this directory).
session_log.not_found: Log file not found. Possible reasons:
session_log.reason_old: The session ran before DC started writing stdout logs (PR that added the feature).
session_log.reason_rotated: Log directory {id} was deleted manually or rotated.
orchestration.confirm_delete:
Delete the run entirely (with messages/nodes/events)?
Files the Orchestrator wrote to disk are NOT deleted.
orchestration.watcher_active: Watcher is active — let's wait a few more seconds…
orchestration.pick_run: Pick a run on the left to see the stage and message graph.
orchestration.or_create: Or start a new task on the right →
orchestration.create_right: Start a task on the right →
orchestration.goal_placeholder: e.g.: «decompose feature X and lay out a plan by stages»
orchestration.chat_voice_hint: Node chat / Voice become active after picking a stage in an open run.
orchestration.confirm_delete_row:
Delete run {id}…?
This also deletes all its messages/nodes/events. Files the Orchestrator wrote to disk are NOT deleted.
orchestration.stale_warning: {n} stale {code} run{plural} — process died, the run stayed open in the DB. Auto-reconcile ticks every 5 min.
tech_debt.empty.title: No markdown files in {dir}
tech_debt.empty.desc: Tech-debt items are written by the {code} slash-command — it spawns claude, which scans the repo for TODO/FIXME/legacy/smells and writes each finding as a markdown file here. Runs weekly on schedule (if cron is enabled) or can be triggered now.
tech_debt.source_hint: Source: {dir} · clicking a status or module → filtered {link} with specific items.
project_dashboard.not_setup_title: Project hasn't been set up «out of the box» yet
project_dashboard.one_click: One button below does it all at once:
project_dashboard.create_dirs: creates {n} folder(s) and writes them into Settings:
project_dashboard.dont_touch: Existing files and already-set settings are not touched. If you want different paths — change them later in
project_not_found.title: Project not found
project_not_found.disabled: This project is disabled in the registry.
project_not_found.check_slug: Check the slug or register the project.
autoconfig.create_desc: I'll create the folder and write the setting {setting}:
autoconfig.change_later: If you want a different path — change it after install in

radar.title: AI Radar
radar.empty: No findings yet. Run the scanner (Wave R2) or seed via scripts/seed_ai_radar.py.
radar.onboarding.title: Watchlist is empty
radar.onboarding.body: Add people and labs to the YAML file at the path below. Without it the scanner has nothing to read.
radar.project.empty: Findings whose relevance_hint contains this project's slug, or which you manually pinned to it, show up here.
radar.scan.done: Scan complete: added {inserted} new signals from {sources} sources with RSS.
radar.scan.hint: Pulls fresh articles via RSS/Atom from watchlist sources that publish a feed.
radar.speak.unsupported: Your browser doesn't support speech (Web Speech API).
